acceptance: `cargo test --workspace` passes. kcat stress script passes. Offset commit/fetch unit tests remain green.
description:
Commit offsets are today stored inside ConsumerGroupManager state. Extract an OffsetStore trait:

- commit(group, topic, partition, offset, metadata)
- fetch(group, topic, partition) -> Option<CommittedOffset>
- delete_group(group)
- capabilities()

Current implementation becomes MemoryOffsetStore. OffsetCommit/OffsetFetch handlers take Arc<dyn OffsetStore>.

In-scope files:
- src/storage/offset_store.rs (new trait + memory impl)
- src/consumer_group/offset_store.rs (refactor: concrete struct implements new trait)
- src/handler/offset_commit.rs, src/handler/offset_fetch.rs (take Arc<dyn OffsetStore>)
- src/server.rs (construction)
Out of scope:
- Postgres impl (separate bead)
- GroupCoordinatorBackend (separate bead)

events.body:
`src/storage/offset_store.rs` was not created — new trait + `MemoryOffsetStore` impl missing.
`src/consumer_group/offset_store.rs` was not refactored to implement the new trait.
`src/handler/offset_commit.rs` and `src/handler/offset_fetch.rs` still use the concrete type rather than `Arc<dyn OffsetStore>`.
`src/server.rs` was not updated for the new construction.
The commit bundles `heimq/target/**` build artifacts that should not be checked in; whoever re-runs this bead should also ensure `target/` stays out of the commit.

result_rev=23eafba4c437887435538797770449a3b3f3c497
base_rev=d40f24df8c9662f9a613bdb796a3e6c3543e2d6c

No changes — bead heimq-04def9c7 ("Extract OffsetStore trait") is already satisfied by the current base commit (caa5be836a49a7894877d49210a8ede4f83ceb1d "checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260426T025857-58781509"), which sits on top of 9362ca3 "chore: execute-bead iteration heimq-04def9c7" — the prior iteration that landed the refactor.

What is done
------------

1. heimq/src/storage/offset_store.rs (in-scope) — present:
   - Defines the `OffsetStore` trait (commit, fetch, fetch_all_for_group, delete_group, capabilities).
   - Defines `CommittedOffset` and `OffsetStoreCapabilities` value types.
   - Re-exported from `heimq/src/storage/mod.rs` as `CommittedOffset`, `OffsetStore`, `OffsetStoreCapabilities`.

2. heimq/src/consumer_group/offset_store.rs (in-scope) — refactored:
   - The previous concrete struct is now `MemoryOffsetStore`, which `impl OffsetStore`.
   - Backed by `DashMap<OffsetKey, CommittedOffset>` (same data as before).
   - Capabilities descriptor `MEMORY_CAPABILITIES { name: "in-memory", durability: None, survives_restart: false }`.
   - Existing unit tests (`test_commit_and_fetch`, `test_fetch_missing`, `test_fetch_all_and_delete_group`, `test_default_store`, `test_capabilities`, `test_trait_object`) all green.

3. heimq/src/handler/offset_commit.rs (in-scope):
   - `pub fn handle(api_version, body, offset_store: &Arc<dyn OffsetStore>) -> Result<OffsetCommitResponse>`.
   - Imports `crate::storage::OffsetStore`.

4. heimq/src/handler/offset_fetch.rs (in-scope):
   - `pub fn handle(_api_version, body, offset_store: &Arc<dyn OffsetStore>) -> Result<OffsetFetchResponse>`.
   - Imports `crate::storage::OffsetStore`.

5. heimq/src/server.rs (in-scope):
   - Constructs `ConsumerGroupManager::new(config.clone())`, which internally builds an
     `Arc<dyn OffsetStore>` via `Arc::new(MemoryOffsetStore::new())` and is also
     overridable via `ConsumerGroupManager::with_offset_store(...)` for tests / future
     durable backends.
   - Router takes `consumer_groups.clone()`, and `consumer_groups.offset_store()` returns
     `&Arc<dyn OffsetStore>` for the offset_commit/offset_fetch handlers.

Verification
------------

- `cargo build` — clean.
- `cargo test --workspace` — 122/122 unit tests pass; 13/14 contract tests pass.
  - The single failure is `contract_api_versions_matches_supported_range` ("api 2 max_version mismatch: left 5, right 7"). This failure reproduces on the unmodified base commit (verified via `git stash && cargo test --test contract contract_api_versions`) and is unrelated to OffsetStore extraction — it is a pre-existing API-versions contract drift between handler advertised ranges and the contract test expectation. Out of scope for this bead.

Why no commit
-------------

The previous review's BLOCKING findings ("offset_store.rs was not created", "handlers still use the concrete type", "server.rs not updated") describe a state that no longer exists — they refer to a state from before iteration commit 9362ca3 was made. Re-running the same refactor would either be a no-op or a destructive churn over already-correct code. There is nothing in scope left to change.

Follow-up suggestion
--------------------

If the reviewer expects the contract `api_versions` mismatch to be fixed, that is a separate bead (out of scope here): bring `handler::api_versions` advertised max for API key 2 (ListOffsets) up to 7, or update the contract expectation, depending on the actual supported range across the codebase.

acceptance: `cargo test --test integration test_rdkafka_auto_commit_interval -- --ignored --nocapture` passes and asserts resume position matches auto-committed offset (within one batch of the last consumed message).
description:
Exercise the enable.auto.commit=true code path with an explicit auto.commit.interval.ms (e.g. 200ms). Consumer reads K messages, waits >interval, closes; fresh consumer in same group should resume after the auto-committed offset. Complements the manual-commit test above.
In-scope files:
- tests/integration.rs (new fn test_rdkafka_auto_commit_interval)

events.body:
APPROVE
The test cleanly exercises the auto-commit interval path using `enable.auto.offset.store=false` to make the committed offset deterministic (only the K'th message is stored). It verifies both the committed-offset bounds and resume semantics (first message offset + payload match), with `auto.offset.reset=latest` on Consumer B to guarantee resume came from the committed offset rather than a reset fallback.

acceptance: All existing tests pass unchanged: `cargo test --workspace` green. `cargo llvm-cov --workspace --all-features --fail-under-lines 100` still passes. `scripts/kcat-stress.sh` with default params still green.
description:
Replace the concrete Storage struct with three traits:

- LogBackend: create_topic, delete_topic, list_topics, topic(name) -> Arc<dyn TopicLog>, capabilities().
- TopicLog: name(), num_partitions(), partition(id) -> Arc<dyn PartitionLog>, config() -> &TopicConfig.
- PartitionLog: append(RecordBatchView, raw_bytes: Option<&[u8]>), read(offset, max_bytes, FetchWait), log_start_offset, high_watermark, truncate_before.

Rename existing Storage/Topic/Partition to MemoryLog/MemoryTopicLog/MemoryPartitionLog and have them implement the traits. Handlers receive Arc<dyn LogBackend> where they currently have Arc<Storage>. Zero behavior change expected.

FetchWait enum: Immediate | LongPoll { min_bytes, max_wait_ms }.

In-scope files:
- src/storage/mod.rs (trait defs + re-exports)
- src/storage/memory.rs (new — rename of current impl)
- src/storage/partition.rs, src/storage/segment.rs, src/storage/topic.rs (updated to implement traits)
- src/handler/*.rs (call-site changes where they hold Arc<Storage>)
- src/server.rs (holds Arc<dyn LogBackend>)
Out of scope:
- OffsetStore and GroupCoordinatorBackend (separate beads)
- URL dispatch (separate bead)
- Any non-memory backend (separate bead)
Depends on: RecordBatchView bead

events.body:
APPROVE
The refactor cleanly introduces `LogBackend` / `TopicLog` / `PartitionLog` traits with the specified method shapes (including `FetchWait::{Immediate, LongPoll}` and `TopicConfig`), renames the in-memory types to `Memory*`, and threads `Arc<dyn LogBackend>` through the server, router, and all handlers. Legacy inherent methods are kept so existing tests continue to work — matching the "zero behavior change" requirement.

Legacy inherent methods are kept so existing tests continue to work — matching the "zero behavior change" requirement.

title: Extract LogBackend + TopicLog + PartitionLog traits

acceptance: All child beads closed. heimq starts with default config using three memory backends and all existing tests pass. One durable backend (Postgres OffsetStore) ships behind a feature flag with integration tests. Capability descriptor drives ApiVersions response.
description:
Make heimq storage pluggable at runtime via URL scheme, without recompiling to swap. Three independent traits — LogBackend, OffsetStore, GroupCoordinatorBackend — each configured separately (default all memory://). Every backend exposes a BackendCapabilities descriptor so the broker can derive ApiVersions honestly and reject unsupported ops early.

Scope notes (from design review with codex):
- Log trait is structured (RecordBatchView + raw-bytes fast-path), NOT byte-opaque, because retention/compaction/timestamps/headers need parsed metadata.
- GroupCoordinatorBackend is richer than a simple load/save KV: it models live coordinator state (members, generations, assignments, sticky session semantics).
- Capability intersection is per-API/per-feature, not one global broker-wide meet. Startup validation for advertised ApiVersions; call-time rejection for per-request limits.
- Proxy-to-real-Kafka mode is explicitly out of scope — it needs a different trait family (UpstreamSession / CoordinatorBackend) and is a different architectural problem.

First durable backend lands as Postgres OffsetStore (smallest, highest value). Additional backends follow under separate beads.
title: Pluggable storage backends

acceptance: `cargo test` green. A manual test (or integration test) with a backend that declares compaction=false verifies no compaction-specific APIs leak into ApiVersions. Existing ApiVersions contract tests still pass for the memory default.
description:
Today SUPPORTED_APIS in src/protocol/mod.rs is static. Now that each backend exposes capabilities, compute the effective advertised ApiVersions at startup by intersecting static protocol support with backend caps (e.g. don't advertise InitProducerId if no backend has transactions; don't advertise OffsetCommit if offsets backend isn't writable).

Per codex review: intersection is per-API, not one global meet — GroupCoordinatorBackend capabilities must not constrain plain Produce/Fetch.

In-scope files:
- src/protocol/mod.rs (SUPPORTED_APIS becomes a computation, or a wrapper that filters)
- src/handler/api_versions.rs (reads computed set)
- src/server.rs (composes on startup)
Depends on: capability descriptor, all three trait extractions
title: Derive ApiVersions response from backend capabilities

acceptance: stress-matrix.sh runs all configured combos and exits 0. If POSTGRES_URL is unset, the postgres row is skipped with a clear SKIPPED line (not FAIL). CI gets a new (optional) job that runs the matrix against a postgres service container.
description:
Run the kcat-stress script against each sensible backend combination and assert behavior is consistent.

Combinations initially:
- log=memory, offsets=memory, groups=memory (default)
- log=memory, offsets=postgres, groups=memory (durability slice)

Script extension: a wrapper scripts/stress-matrix.sh that loops over combos, setting HEIMQ_STORAGE_LOG / HEIMQ_STORAGE_OFFSETS / HEIMQ_STORAGE_GROUPS env vars per iteration, running the existing kcat-stress.sh at moderate volume (e.g. 5 topics x 10k msgs).

In-scope files:
- scripts/stress-matrix.sh (new)
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plan/test-plan.md (add Phase 6: backend matrix)
Depends on: postgres backend bead
title: Integration test matrix: kcat-stress against each backend combo

acceptance: `cargo test --test integration test_rdkafka_fetch_long_poll -- --ignored --nocapture` passes. Both timing assertions hold within a reasonable tolerance (e.g. 300ms < poll_a < 800ms; poll_b < 300ms after produce).
description:
Consumer with fetch.min.bytes=1024 and fetch.max.wait.ms=500 polls an empty-ish topic. Case A: no new data — poll returns after ~500ms with empty batch (not 0ms, not hung). Case B: producer writes enough bytes mid-wait — poll returns immediately after threshold reached.
In-scope files:
- tests/integration.rs (new fn test_rdkafka_fetch_long_poll)
title: e2e: long-poll fetch respects fetch.min.bytes + fetch.max.wait.ms

acceptance: `cargo test --lib storage::record_batch_view` passes with tests that construct a view from a kafka_protocol RecordBatch and iterate records. Existing tests unaffected.
description:
The log trait needs to accept parsed batch metadata so backends can implement retention by timestamp, compaction by key, header-aware filtering, etc. Today produce handler (src/handler/produce.rs) and fetch handler (src/handler/fetch.rs) pass raw record-batch bytes through to Storage::append.

Add a RecordBatchView type that carries: producer_id, producer_epoch, base_offset (placeholder), base_timestamp, max_timestamp, is_transactional, is_control, compression codec, record count, borrowed iterator over records (offset_delta, timestamp_delta, key, value, headers), and the original &[u8] for fast-path pass-through.

No decoding work beyond what produce handler already does via kafka_protocol::records — we wrap that result.

In-scope files:
- src/storage/record_batch_view.rs (new)
- src/storage/mod.rs (re-export)
Out of scope:
- Calling it from handler/produce.rs (separate bead)
- Changing storage API (separate bead)

events.body:
APPROVE
Clean, scoped implementation that matches the spec exactly: a structured view over a decoded kafka_protocol RecordBatch with all requested batch-level metadata, a borrowed record iterator exposing deltas and headers, and the original bytes retained for pass-through. Tests cover the happy path, compression, and error. One minor note (not blocking): `base_offset`/`base_timestamp` are computed as `min` over records rather than re…
artifact: .ddx/executions/20260424T013155-e283543d/reviewer-stream.log

success
Clean, scoped implementation that matches the spec exactly: a structured view over a decoded kafka_protocol RecordBatch with all requested batch-level metadata, a borrowed record iterator exposing deltas and headers, and the original bytes retained for pass-through. Tests cover the happy path, compression, and error. One minor note (not blocking): `base_offset`/`base_timestamp` are computed as `min` over records rather than read from the batch header, which is defensive but slightly at odds with the doc comment saying base_offset comes "from the batch header"; behavior is still correct for well-formed batches.

The test faithfully implements resume-from-committed semantics: producer sends 10 messages, consumer A commits after 4 and drops, consumer B joins the same group with `auto.offset.reset=latest` and must see exactly messages 4..9. The `latest` reset combined with produce-before-B-subscribes is a clean isolation of resume semantics from reset behavior, and the drain loop guards against over-delivery. Assertions pin first payload…

success

Assertions pin first payload, last payload, and count.

title: e2e: per-partition ordering under concurrent produce

acceptance: All child beads closed. `cargo test --test integration -- --ignored` passes cleanly. Test plan Phase 5 checkboxes all marked.
description: Close the rdkafka-driven producer/consumer gaps identified in docs/helix/03-test/test-plan/test-plan.md Phase 5. Scope is tests/integration.rs only — no contract, unit, or property work. Prioritization per external review (codex): group correctness > offset behavior > multi-partition integrity > partition selection > fetch wait > error paths > concurrency/soak > client-surface (headers/compression/seek/pause). Test plan section is authoritative; this epic tracks its completion. All child beads add tests that exercise heimq via a real rdkafka client against an in-process TestServer (see existing pattern in tests/integration.rs).
title: Phase 5: rdkafka producer+consumer e2e coverage

acceptance: `cargo test --test integration test_rdkafka_compression_codecs -- --ignored --nocapture` passes for all four codecs. If a codec is unsupported, test documents and asserts a clear error rather than silent corruption.
description:
For each of compression.type = gzip, snappy, lz4, zstd: producer encodes a batch, consumer decodes and asserts payload equality. Exercises the broker's RecordBatch decode paths for each codec (heimq must re-read batch metadata but the compressed payload itself is passed through opaque — confirm behavior).
In-scope files:
- tests/integration.rs (new fn test_rdkafka_compression_codecs, parameterized over codecs)
- Cargo.toml dev-dependencies (enable rdkafka compression features if needed)
title: e2e: compression codec round-trips (gzip, snappy, lz4, zstd)

acceptance: `cargo test --test integration test_rdkafka_explicit_partition_target -- --ignored --nocapture` passes and asserts the message appears only on the targeted partition.
description:
rdkafka FutureProducer allows specifying a partition in FutureRecord::partition(). Produce to partition 2 of a 4-partition topic, consume only partition 2 via assign() rather than subscribe(), assert the message is present. Ensures heimq honors explicit partition selection bypassing the partitioner.
In-scope files:
- tests/integration.rs (new fn test_rdkafka_explicit_partition_target)

events.body:

Test cleanly exercises explicit partition targeting: the producer-side assertion verifies rdkafka honored the partition override, and the `assign()`-only consumer proves isolation from other partitions. Uses existing `TestServer::start_with_partitions` and `rdkafka_consumer` helpers, and follows the `#[ignore]` convention of the surrounding rdkafka tests.

description:
Produce N messages to topic T. Two consumer groups G1 and G2 each subscribe to T independently. Assert each group receives the full message set, and that committing in G1 does not affect G2's position (committed offsets are group-scoped, not topic-scoped).

- tests/integration.rs (new fn test_rdkafka_independent_consumer_groups)

acceptance: `cargo test --lib storage::capabilities` passes with a basic test constructing a BackendCapabilities and asserting field defaults. `cargo build --release` passes.
description:
Introduce a BackendCapabilities struct exposing what a backend can do: durability (None|Snapshot|WalFsync), atomic_append scope (Partition|Topic|Cluster), survives_restart, compaction, transactions, idempotent_producer, timestamps support, headers, compression codecs, max message/batch/partitions, fetch_wait support, read_your_writes, retention modes, truncate, name, version. Also related enums.

At this step the struct is used by nothing — purely additive — but its shape is the contract future backends will implement. Struct lives in src/storage/capabilities.rs; re-exported from src/storage/mod.rs.

In-scope files:
- src/storage/capabilities.rs (new)
- src/storage/mod.rs (re-export)
Out of scope:
- Wiring into ApiVersions (separate bead)
- Implementing the capability for the existing in-memory storage (separate bead after trait extraction)

acceptance: `cargo test --test integration test_rdkafka_auto_offset_reset_earliest_vs_latest -- --ignored --nocapture` passes with both branches asserted.
description:
Pre-populate topic T with M messages before any consumer exists. Start consumer C1 in new group G1 with auto.offset.reset=earliest — must receive all M messages. Start consumer C2 in new group G2 with auto.offset.reset=latest — must receive zero existing messages but see subsequent produces.
In-scope files:
- tests/integration.rs (new fn test_rdkafka_auto_offset_reset_earliest_vs_latest)

events.body:
APPROVE
Test cleanly covers both branches of the acceptance criteria: earliest consumer drains the pre-populated backlog, and a fresh-group latest consumer sees nothing from before it joined but catches subsequent produces. Uses a 3-second settle window to ensure C2's partition position is set to high-watermark before new produces, which is the right guard against a race.

acceptance: `cargo test --test integration test_rdkafka_produce_consume_soak -- --ignored --nocapture` passes within a time budget and all three invariants hold.
description:
Producer and consumer(s) run concurrently for a fixed batch size (e.g. 10k messages total across >=3 partitions). Assert: (a) consumed count == produced count, (b) no duplicate (topic, partition, offset) tuples, (c) per-partition offsets strictly monotonic. Time-bounded (e.g. <30s) so it fits in CI.
In-scope files:
- tests/integration.rs (new fn test_rdkafka_produce_consume_soak)
title: e2e: produce-while-consume soak

acceptance: `cargo test --features backend-postgres` green. Integration test: commit an offset via OffsetCommit handler backed by PostgresOffsetStore, restart the TestServer, fetch the same offset, assert it survived. Test gracefully skips if no postgres URL is configured in CI.
description:
First durable backend. Stores committed offsets in a Postgres database. Feature-gated: `--features backend-postgres`.

Schema:
  CREATE TABLE heimq_committed_offsets (
    group_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    topic TEXT NOT NULL,
    partition INT NOT NULL,
    committed_offset BIGINT NOT NULL,
    metadata TEXT,
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    PRIMARY KEY (group_id, topic, partition)
  );

Implementation:
- sqlx or tokio-postgres dependency, gated by feature.
- PostgresOffsetStore::connect(url) returns Arc<dyn OffsetStore>.
- BackendCapabilities: durability=WalFsync, survives_restart=true, transactions=false (this trait doesn't expose them), read_your_writes=true.
- URL scheme: postgres://user:pw@host:port/db?schema=heimq
- Schema initialize() idempotent at startup.

In-scope files:
- Cargo.toml (feature flag + sqlx dep)
- src/storage/postgres_offsets.rs (new)
- src/storage/dispatch.rs (register postgres://)
- tests/postgres_offsets.rs (integration test, uses a containerized postgres via testcontainers or skip if env var HEIMQ_PG_TEST_URL unset)
Depends on: OffsetStore trait, URL dispatch
title: Postgres OffsetStore backend (feature-gated)

description:
Producer attempts to send a message larger than the broker's configured max. Assert a bounded, observable failure (error on delivery report or client-side rejection), not a hang or partial produce. If heimq has no enforced max, document that — if it does enforce one, assert the boundary behavior.

acceptance: `cargo test` green. Starting heimq with default config uses three memory backends. Starting with an unknown scheme (e.g. `log = "weird://"`) fails at startup with a clear error. kcat stress passes.
description:
Add a [storage] config block with three URL fields (log, offsets, groups), all defaulting to memory://. Introduce a dispatch fn per trait that parses the URL scheme and returns Arc<dyn ...>. Default config produces three memory backends; existing behavior unchanged.

Only the memory:// scheme resolves at this step. Unknown schemes return a configuration error at startup (fail fast, not at first request).

In-scope files:
- src/config.rs (new StorageConfig struct, feeding three URLs)
- src/storage/dispatch.rs (new — scheme -> Arc<dyn LogBackend>, OffsetStore, GroupCoordinatorBackend)
- src/server.rs (construct backends via dispatch)
- tests/config tests
Out of scope:
- Postgres or any non-memory scheme (separate bead)
Depends on: LogBackend + OffsetStore + GroupCoordinatorBackend traits (beads 3, 4, 5)
title: URL-scheme config + runtime dispatch for storage backends

acceptance: `cargo test --test integration test_rdkafka_group_multi_partition_delivery -- --ignored --nocapture` passes. Test asserts: (a) set of consumed (partition, offset) pairs == set of produced, (b) no partition assigned to two members at the same time during steady state.
description:
Create a multi-partition topic (>=3 partitions), produce N messages (>=300) with distinct keys, start a consumer group with >=2 members, assert every produced message is consumed exactly once across the group and that no two members claim the same partition concurrently. Supersedes the earlier 'exactly-once' framing — Kafka groups do not guarantee exactly-once; assertion is no gaps + no duplicate ownership.
In-scope files:
- tests/integration.rs (new #[tokio::test] fn test_rdkafka_group_multi_partition_delivery)
Out of scope:
- tests/contract.rs — contract layer is covered
- Any src/ changes — this should work against current broker behavior

acceptance: `cargo test --test integration test_rdkafka_group_rebalance_on -- --ignored --nocapture` runs both tests and asserts final partition assignment maps entirely to the surviving member.
description:
Two scenarios in one test file: (1) member calls unsubscribe()/close() → LeaveGroup → remaining member takes over all partitions; (2) member stops heartbeating and session timeout expires → coordinator evicts member → remaining member takes over. Existing test_rdkafka_consumer_rebalance_on_new_member covers the join direction; this covers leave.
In-scope files:
- tests/integration.rs (new fns test_rdkafka_group_rebalance_on_graceful_leave, test_rdkafka_group_rebalance_on_session_timeout)

events.body:
APPROVE
Both scenarios implemented in a single test file as specified, with clear assertions that the surviving member's final assignment equals the full partition set `{0,1,2}`. Timeouts and poll cadences are reasonable for the respective leave modes (30s for graceful, 45s for session-timeout eviction).

title: e2e: rebalance on member leave (graceful + session timeout)

acceptance: `cargo test --workspace` green. kcat stress script group-consumer flow passes with default stagger.
description:
Unlike a simple load/save KV, the group coordinator is live state: members, generations, assignments, sticky session behavior, rebalance state machine. Per codex review, this trait must be richer than a snapshot store.

Trait surface (initial):
- join_group(group, member, protocols) -> JoinResult { generation, member_id, leader, ... }
- sync_group(group, generation, member, assignments) -> SyncResult
- heartbeat(group, generation, member) -> HeartbeatResult
- leave_group(group, member) -> LeaveResult
- commit_offset / fetch_offset could either be here OR in OffsetStore — for the first cut, keep OffsetStore separate (already its own bead).
- capabilities()

Memory impl wraps current ConsumerGroupManager logic; no behavior change.

In-scope files:
- src/consumer_group/mod.rs (trait + memory impl wiring)
- src/consumer_group/coordinator.rs (refactor)
- src/handler/{join_group,sync_group,heartbeat,leave_group}.rs (take Arc<dyn GroupCoordinatorBackend>)
Out of scope:
- Any durable group backend (separate future bead)
- Rebalance-delay fix (separate bead, filed as flakiness bug)

Diff under review contains only ddx execution evidence files (manifest.json, result.json). No source changes to consumer_group, coordinator, or handlers are present, so the acceptance criteria cannot be evaluated from this diff.

Bead: heimq-fb3e5ca8 — Extract GroupCoordinatorBackend trait

Status: no source changes required this pass.

What is done
------------
The bead is already fully implemented in commit be214d9
("feat(consumer_group): extract GroupCoordinatorBackend trait
[heimq-fb3e5ca8]"), which is part of the base for this re-execution
(base-rev 4e34094 = checkpoint after be214d9).

That commit delivers the contract spelled out in the bead:

  * heimq/src/consumer_group/backend.rs
      - trait GroupCoordinatorBackend (Send + Sync) with:
          join_group(JoinRequest) -> JoinResult
          sync_group(SyncRequest) -> SyncResult
          heartbeat(group, generation, member) -> HeartbeatResult
          leave_group(group, &[member_id]) -> LeaveResult
          capabilities() -> &GroupCoordinatorCapabilities
      - request/result structs (JoinRequest, JoinMember, JoinResult,
        SyncRequest, SyncResult, HeartbeatResult, LeaveResult)
      - GroupCoordinatorCapabilities { name, version, durability,
        survives_restart, multi_node }

  * heimq/src/consumer_group/mod.rs
      - in-memory ConsumerGroupManager implements
        GroupCoordinatorBackend with no behaviour change (wraps the
        existing coordinator state machine).

  * heimq/src/handler/{join_group,sync_group,heartbeat,leave_group}.rs
      - now depend on &dyn GroupCoordinatorBackend instead of the
        concrete ConsumerGroupManager.

  * heimq/src/protocol/router.rs and heimq/src/handler/tests.rs
      - updated to thread the trait through.

OffsetStore is intentionally left on its own trait per the bead's
"keep OffsetStore separate" direction; offset_commit / offset_fetch
handlers were not touched.

Why the prior reviewer marked REVIEW:BLOCK
------------------------------------------
The diff under review at that point only contained ddx execution
evidence files (manifest.json, result.json) for an iteration that
ran *after* be214d9 had already merged the source changes. There was
nothing source-shaped left to ship for this bead, so the diff
legitimately had no consumer_group / handler edits — they were
already on the base branch. This is a bookkeeping situation, not a
missing-implementation situation.

Acceptance criteria check
-------------------------
AC1: `cargo test --workspace` green.
  Ran in this worktree. All consumer-group, coordinator, handler, and
  protocol tests pass. One unrelated pre-existing failure remains:

      tests/contract.rs ::
        contract_api_versions_matches_supported_range
      (api 2 max_version: expected 7, got 5)

  This failure is reproducible on the base commit (4e34094) with the
  worktree fully clean — it is in api_versions, which this bead does
  not touch, and it predates be214d9. It belongs to a separate
  api-versions bead, not this one.

AC2: kcat stress script group-consumer flow passes with default
     stagger.
  Cannot execute kcat in this sandbox; the in-process
  contract_consumer_group_lifecycle / contract_join_group_new_member /
  contract_sync_group_leader / contract_heartbeat_active_member /
  contract_leave_group_success tests all pass against the
  trait-routed handlers, which exercise the same join → sync →
  heartbeat → leave path the kcat script drives.

What a follow-up would need
---------------------------
Nothing at the source level for this bead. If the reviewer wants the
acceptance gate to flip green automatically, the api_versions
mismatch needs its own bead (bump api 2 max to 7 in the supported
range, or adjust the contract test expectation to match the
implementation — out of scope here).
